target.name: Q-Factor
target.founder: Nir Davidson, Ofer Firstenberg, Yoav Sagi, Guy Raz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Q-Factor is a Seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised $24M to date. The broader quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the quantum computing universe—no Seed-stage peers with sufficient valuation data exist to support a reliable median benchmark. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression analysis, which applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next financing stage. Using total capital raised ($24M) as a proxy for entry valuation, this method implies a post-Seed valuation of $60M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M (median $60M), reflecting typical variance around the stage-progression step-up. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-peer financial data to validate the 2.5x multiplier in quantum computing specifically, and whether Q-Factor's capital efficiency or technology differentiation relative to later-stage peers (IonQ, Quantinuum) would justify upside or downside adjustment. Additional data on burn rate, technology milestones, and IP positioning would refine this range materially.
